messages.content:
I am not able to get github education pack it messages me as : "You are unlikely to be verified until you have completed your GitHub user profile with your full name as it appears in your academic affiliation document plus a short bio.

Please consider using your device camera to submit academic affiliation documents. Uploaded images are more easily manipulated and are therefore less trustworthy."

As an AI language model, I do not have access to your credentials or personal information. However, if you are facing trouble getting verified for the GitHub Education Pack, then you need to ensure that your GitHub user profile is complete with your full name and academic affiliation details along with a short bio. Additionally, it is recommended that you submit your academic documents via the device camera rather than uploading them as images because uploaded images can be easily manipulated and less trustworthy. If you are still facing issues, you can reach out to the GitHub support team for further assistance.
I did change my name and add short bio what should I do ?

If you have already updated your full name and added a short bio to your GitHub profile as it appears in your academic affiliation document, then you should try submitting the academic affiliation document through your device camera instead of uploading an image. This will help to ensure that your documents are more securely verified. If you continue to face issues or receive the same message, you may want to contact GitHub support for further assistance.
one time I uploaded a nice picture of my student card, then a webcam photo of my student card then an image of my transcript

If you have already uploaded a picture of your student card, a webcam photo of your student card, and an image of your transcript and are still receiving the message that you are unlikely to be verified until you have completed your GitHub user profile, then it's possible that there may be some other issue. 

In that case, you can try reaching out to the GitHub support team to ask why your verification process is not proceeding. They may be able to provide you with additional guidance or troubleshoot specific issues that are preventing your verification. Make sure to explain your situation in detail and provide all relevant information about your attempts to verify your account.

It says Your application cannot be reviewed until you fix the following:
You are unlikely to be verified until you have completed your GitHub user profile with your full name as it appears in your academic affiliation document plus a short bio.

As the message suggests, it seems that your GitHub user profile is missing some information that is required for verification. Make sure that you have entered your full name exactly as it appears in your academic affiliation document along with a short bio. Double-check all your profile details to ensure that everything is correct and up to date.

Once you have updated your profile details, you can try submitting your application for the GitHub Education Pack again. If you continue to experience issues, contact the GitHub support team for further assistance. Be sure to explain the issue in detail and provide all relevant information to help them understand the problem and assist you better.
what should I write in short bio

Your short bio should be a brief description of yourself and your interests. This can include information such as your field of study, your academic background, any relevant skills or experience you may have, and your goals and aspirations.

Try to keep your bio concise and to the point, highlighting your key strengths and accomplishments. You can also consider adding some personal details such as hobbies or interests to make your profile more engaging. Remember to keep your bio professional and appropriate for a business or academic context.

Here is an example short bio:

"Computer Science student with a keen interest in artificial intelligence and machine learning. Experienced in programming languages such as Python, C++, and Java. Passionate about developing innovative software solutions and exploring the latest advancements in the field. In my free time, I enjoy hiking and playing guitar."

ok I got it, but now when I go to name space I type in the primary e mail but it says "It looks like your email is not associated with an eligible university. Send us a request and we'll consider your school!"

If you are getting a message that suggests your email is not associated with an eligible university, then it is possible that your educational institution is not currently eligible for the GitHub Education Pack.

In this case, you can try sending a request to GitHub Education to have your institution considered for eligibility. To do this, click on the "Send us a request" link and provide the necessary information such as the name of your institution and its location.

GitHub Education will review your request and determine whether your institution meets the eligibility criteria for the Education Pack. If your institution is approved, you'll be able to access the full range of benefits offered by the Education Pack.

If your request is not approved or you continue to face issues with your application, you can contact the GitHub Education support team for further assistance.

no I got GitHub Education Pack. I am trying to get a free .me domain. it says in github education pack: "About Namecheap

Affordable registration, hosting, and domain management

Benefit

1 year domain name registration on the .me TLD.

Virtual Events
Benefit

1 SSL certificate free for 1 year."

If you have already received the GitHub Education Pack and are looking to claim the free .me domain provided by Namecheap, you can follow the steps below:

1. Visit the GitHub Education Pack website and select the Namecheap benefit.
2. Follow the instructions on the Namecheap website to create an account or log in to your existing account.
3. Use the provided coupon code to register for a free .me domain for one year.
4. Complete the registration process by providing the required information and following any additional instructions.

Once you have completed the registration process, you will be able to use your new .me domain for your personal or academic projects.
I am at https://education.github.com/pack, I can't select any benefits

If you are unable to select any benefits on the GitHub Education Pack website, it might be due to the following reasons:

1. You may not have completed the verification process. To access the benefits, you need to verify your eligibility by submitting your academic affiliation documents.

2. Your verification may be still under review. It may take some time for the verification process to complete. Once you are verified, you should see the eligible benefits available for activation.

3. There may be a problem with your account. Try logging out and logging back in to your GitHub Education Pack account.

4. You may have already activated the benefits. Double-check if you have already activated the benefit you are looking for or not.

If none of the above solutions work, try contacting GitHub Education support to solve the issue.
I got email saying "✨ Welcome to Global Campus" does that not mean I got education pack or should I claim it somewhere else ?

Getting an email from Global Campus does not necessarily mean that you have received the GitHub Education Pack. Global Campus is a different organization that focuses on various educational initiatives. However, you can check the eligibility status of your application for the GitHub Education Pack by logging in to your GitHub Education Pack account. If your application has been approved, you should be able to claim the available benefits by following the instructions provided on the website. 

If you are still unsure about your eligibility or unable to access the benefits, you can directly contact the GitHub Education support team. They can assist you with any issues related to your application or account.
